text: AI as a Digital Determinant of Health

text: Staff Training Module
textPreview: Staff Training Module

text: AI Playbook and Toolkit for Public Health Departments
textPreview: AI Playbook and Toolkit for Public Health Departments

alt: AI Playbook for Public Health logo

text:
Welcome participants to the AI as a Digital Determinant of Health lesson.
Explain that this deck is designed for staff training, leadership orientation, governance preparation, or cross-program discussion.
Frame the topic in plain language: AI can shape access to services, quality of information, exposure to misinformation, eligibility decisions, language access, public benefits, and community trust.
Invite participants to think about where this topic already appears in their department's workflows, vendor tools, data systems, communications, or planning conversations.
AI PLAYBOOK AND TOOLKIT

text: Public health value
textPreview: Public health value

text: AI can influence whether people receive understandable information, timely services, fair prioritization, language access, or meaningful alternatives. Departments should treat AI-supported workflows as part of the digital environment that.
textPreview: AI can influence whether people receive understandable information, timely services, fair prioritization, language access, or meaningful ...

text: Guardrail
textPreview: Guardrail

text: AI-enabled systems can widen inequities if digital access, data representation, and community context are not examined early.
textPreview: AI-enabled systems can widen inequities if digital access, data representation, and community context are not examined early.

text: AI as a Digital Determinant of Health
textPreview: AI as a Digital Determinant of Health

text:
Use this slide in the AI as a Digital Determinant of Health training module.
Use this slide to explain the practical public health reason for learning this topic before AI tools are approved or deployed.
Connect the discussion back to the playbook: this topic supports Plays 1, 4, 7, 13 and Tools 3, 22, 19.
Emphasize the core guardrail: AI-enabled systems can widen inequities if digital access, data representation, and community context are not examined early.
Ask participants how this topic could affect their own program area, staff roles, community relationships, data, or public communications.

text: Learning objectives
textPreview: Learning objectives

text: By the end of this lesson, staff should be able to:
textPreview: By the end of this lesson, staff should be able to:

text:
• Explain AI as a Digital Determinant of Health in plain language.
• Identify appropriate public health uses and limits.
• Describe human review, governance, equity, privacy, and monitoring expectations.
• Connect the topic to the related plays and tools.

text:
Use this slide in the AI as a Digital Determinant of Health training module.
Walk through the objectives and connect each one to a practical implementation decision.
Connect the discussion back to the playbook: this topic supports Plays 1, 4, 7, 13 and Tools 3, 22, 19.
Explain that participants do not need technical expertise to use this material. The goal is enough shared understanding to participate in governance, readiness assessment, use case review, procurement, deployment, or monitoring.
AI PLAYBOOK AND TOOLKIT
textPreview: AI PLAYBOOK AND TOOLKIT

text: Module overview
textPreview: Module overview

text: AI can become a digital determinant of health because it shapes how people receive information, access services, are prioritized for outreach, experience language access, or are affected by automated decisions. Departments should examine whether AI-supported systems make services more reachable and fair, or whether they create new barriers.

text: Action Steps
textPreview: Action Steps

text: Include digital access and language access in use case prioritization. Test public-facing outputs with affected users before launch. Monitor service access, complaints, correction requests, and subgroup outcomes after deployment.

text:
Use this slide in the AI as a Digital Determinant of Health training module.
Use this slide to make the topic concrete through public health workflow examples.
Connect the discussion back to the playbook: this topic supports Plays 1, 4, 7, 13 and Tools 3, 22, 19.
For each example, ask: What problem is being solved? What data would be used? Who reviews the AI output? What decision remains human? What could go wrong?

text: Discussion questions
textPreview: Discussion questions

text: Who might be excluded by the data, interface, language, channel, or workflow? Does the AI-supported process create or reduce barriers to services? What non-digital or non-AI alternative is available for important services?

textPreview: Training exercise

text:
Use this slide in the AI as a Digital Determinant of Health training module.
Use this slide for a facilitated activity near the end of the lesson.
Connect the discussion back to the playbook: this topic supports Plays 1, 4, 7, 13 and Tools 3, 22, 19.
Give participants time to work individually or in small groups. Ask them to produce a practical artifact that can feed into readiness assessment, governance review, use case prioritization, or implementation planning.
Expected takeaways include: Digital access impact review, Language and accessibility mitigation plan, Alternate pathway documentation.
